data.body:
==================== EPIC gnu-whale-ichor (e68d) — TRANSITION HANDOFF ====================
STATUS: Epic NOT done. All 8 original leaves were implemented + landed-green on Gerrit, but
the DELIVERED DESIGN DIVERGED from the user's APPROVED epic ACs on 4 points. The user
REJECTED the diverged design. DO NOT SUBMIT/LAND the current stack. Transition stories are
filed under the epic to move the implementation to the approved design.

--- WHY THE DIVERGENCE HAPPENED (own it) ---
I (orchestrator) authored each child-story's PLAN. Those plans drifted from the epic's
approved ACs. The plan-review gate PASSED them, but plan-review validates a plan's INTERNAL
consistency, NOT fidelity to the parent epic's ACs — so the drift wasn't caught until the
EPIC's completion-verifier flagged AC8/AC9 at epic-close time. The 4 deltas below are the gap.

--- THE 4 DELTAS (approved epic AC  vs  what I delivered) ---
1) AUTHORSHIP PAYLOAD (bde5): approved = in-toto Statement, subject binds {event_uuid,
   content_hash}, DSSE payloadType application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json, PAE-signed.
   DELIVERED = signs the event's RAW canonical bytes in DSSE (no in-toto Statement). Low stakes.
2) KEY VALIDITY / ERA MODEL (e165) — SECURITY-CRITICAL: approved = git-commit-ancestry over
   tickets-branch commit SHAs (key record {pubkey, added_at_commit, revoked_at_commit}; event
   valid iff its commit descends from added_at_commit AND NOT from revoked_at_commit, via
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor`). DELIVERED = HLC-EPOCH (ordinal position in the reducer's
   canonical replay; {public_key, added_epoch, revoked_epoch}, keyring_epoch cursor). WEAKNESS:
   HLC ordering is partly author-assigned, so a malicious clone holding a later-revoked key can
   write an event with a backdated HLC that sorts BEFORE the revocation and verifies as valid —
   the merge-gate (3183) computes era the same way so it does NOT catch it. The immutable commit
   DAG closes this (can't forge ancestry without forging SHAs). THIS IS THE MAIN REWORK.
3) SNAPSHOT LEDGER (3183): approved = {event_uuid, content_hash, signature, signer_pubkey,
   position(commit, intra_idx)} — self-contained, verifiable in isolation. DELIVERED =
   {event_uuid, author_id, author_sig, epoch} — re-resolves the identity keyring to verify.
4) VERDICT (3183): approved = distinct `key_not_valid_at_era` verdict +
   verified/unverified/unsigned display grouping (+ enum in verify_signature_result.schema.json).
   DELIVERED = folds revoked-key case into bad-signature/unknown-author (a revoked-key event is
   indistinguishable from a forged one). Also epic AC1 named create_placeholder(...); I built
   ensure_identity_for (functionally equivalent, idempotent) — add the named alias.

NOTE: ddbe(entity), 4e9d(attribution), 264f(Jira outbound), 2f13(inbound ghost) are CORRECT/
NOT in dispute. Only bde5, e165, 3183, 4b5b(docs) diverge.

--- TRANSITION STORIES (new children of epic e68d, ordered TS1->TS2->TS3->TS4) ---
TS1 c96d-58b0-eede-4569 (thistly-undesirous-rail): authorship -> in-toto Statement (+ create_placeholder alias). transitions bde5.
TS2 09f9-f66c-9748-48e5 (halfcrazed-sylphlike-salmon): key validity -> git-commit-ancestry (replaces HLC-epoch). transitions e165. HARDEST. depends_on TS1.
TS3 117b-2496-b0a9-4723 (shagreen-nonorganic-dorado): ledger schema {content_hash,signature,signer_pubkey,position} + key_not_valid_at_era verdict. transitions 3183. depends_on TS1,TS2.
TS4 b134-7545-94fd-4746 (sopping-cloned-dragon): docs/ADR rewrite to approved design. transitions 4b5b. (do last, after TS1-3.)
Each story's DESCRIPTION holds its full plan + ACs (= the epic's original ACs for that area).
Core TS2 challenge: event->commit-SHA resolution (commit SHA is git metadata, not in event JSON;
resolve via `git log --diff-filter=A --format=%H -- <event-file>` for the introducing commit).

--- HOW TO EXECUTE THE TRANSITION (next session) ---
- Each transition story REWORKS the delivered code, so it AMENDS the corresponding Gerrit change
  (bde5=561, e165=563, 3183=580, 4b5b=581) — NOT new changes. Use the held-out TDD loop per
  rebar-implement. Then land the CORRECTED stack.
- The diverged bde5/e165 changes are MID-STACK (264f/2f13 build on them), so the rework must
  amend in place and re-rebase the upper stack (see git surgery notes below).
- Re-run the epic completion-verifier only after TS1-4 land; it will check the approved ACs.
